case_title: MABEL LIN VS. R.J. MCGLENNON COMPANY, INC et al

docket_entries.description: 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LIN, MABEL AS TO DEFENDANT R.J. MCGLENNON COMPANY, INC. MCGLENNON, MICHAEL DOES 1 TO 100

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGLENNON'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. 29 U.S.C. SEC. 2611(4)(A)(I) PROVIDES THAT EMPLOYEES CAN BE HELD INDIVIDUALLY LIABLE. (SEE MITCHELL V. CHAPMAN (6TH CIR. 2003) 343 F.3D 811, 827.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGLENNON WAS PLAINTIFF'S SUPERVISOR AND EXERCISED CONTROL OVER HER ABILITY TO TAKE LEAVE. (FAC, PAR. 23.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Defendant Michael Mcglennon
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL H. KIM, TEL. NO. (213) 738-6005 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGLENNON'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Denied. 29 U.S.C. sec. 2611(4)(A)(i) provides that employees can be held individually liable. (See Mitchell v. Chapman (6th Cir. 2003) 343 F.3d 811, 827.) The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") sufficiently alleges that Defendant Michael McGlennon was Plaintiff's supervisor and exercised control over her ability to take leave. (FAC, par. 23.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
